en: How do you go to the block-house?
I drive to the block-house.
How does he go to the hospital?
He walks to the hospital.
How do you go to Penghu?
I go to Penghu by plane.
How do you go to Penghu?
I go to Penghu by boat.
How do you go home?
I go home by MRT.
How does Paicʉ go to school?
Paicʉ goes to school by train.
How does Pani go to church?
Pani goes to church by bicycle.
Do you go to school by MRT?
No, I go to school by bus.
Do you go to school by bicycle?
Yes, I go to school by bicycle.

ab: tupara kara kasu kao-tie mukusa Kao-siung?
en: Do you go to Kaohsiung by High Speed Rail?

ab: ʉʉ’ʉ, tupara ku kao-tie mukusa Kaosiung.
en: Yes, I go to Kaohsiung by High Speed Rail.

ab: mukusa kasu Taitung, tukisia kara? nukai tuhikoki?
ch: A:你去台東搭火車還是搭飛機?
en: Are you going to Taitung by train or by plane?

ab: tuhikoki ku, mara’an tuhikoki sua na kisia.
ch: B:我搭飛機，搭飛機比搭火車快。
en: I will take the plane because it is faster than the train.

ab: mukusa kasu kiukai, tupasu kara? nukai ’unting kuruma?
ch: A:你去教會搭公車還是開車?
en: Are you going to drive to the church or take the bus?

ab: ’unting ku kuruma mukusa kiukai, maraa munasʉ.
ch: B:我開車去教會，開車比搭公車方便。
en: I will drive to the church because it is more convenient than the bus.

ab: aracan kara tanasa mu sua tamia’aa cau-sʉ?
en: Is the market close to your house?

ab: ka’anʉ aracan, tu’utupai ku mukusa tamia’aa cau-sʉ.
ch: B:不近，我都騎摩托車去超市。
en: No, it is not, so I always ride the motorcycle to the market.

ab: aracan kara tarisinata mu sua yu-ci?
en: Is the post office close to the school?
Yes, it is within walking distance.
Is the restaurant close to the station?

ab: aranai tatuparaa kisia. rima vungu makacʉkʉna cu.
ch: B:出車站再走五分鐘就到了。
en: It only takes five minutes after you walk out of the station.
